title: Dissociation
definition: When a soluble ionic compound dissolves, it separates into free ions rather than staying intact: $\text{Na}_3\text{PO}_4(aq) \rightarrow 3\,\text{Na}^+(aq) + \text{PO}_4^{3-}(aq)$. Treating a strong electrolyte as completely dissociated is a disclosed idealization — exact inside the model, not proven — and it is what lets the complete-ionic equation show every dissolved salt as its separate ions.
